name: spacetrace
documentation:
# Space Trace
Spacetrace is a simple, lightweight, low-abstraction tool for visualizing astrodynamic trajectories.
Spacetrace is agnostic of the physics, coordinate systems and the tools to generate the data. It's sole purpose is to
plot trajectories and inspect them visually.

This tool should be used similarly to matplotlib's 3d plotting tool, but optimized for interactivity and inspection, 
as well as drawing large trajectories at a smooth framerate.

<!--![Screenshot 1](/images/img1.png) -->
![Screenshot 2](/images/img2.png)

## Installation
Spacetrace is a standard python package available on pypi:
```bash
pip install spacetrace
```
Spacetrace only depends on numpy as data interface and pyray for drawing.

## Usage

The most basic usecase is as follows:
```py
    import spacetrace

    scene = spacetrace.Scene()
    scene.add_trajectory(epochs, states)
    scene.add_static_body(0, 0, 0, radius=6.7e6, name='Earth', color='blue')
    spacetrace.show_scene(scene)
```
where `states` is a numpy array of size N x 3 or N x 6 and  epochs is a numpy array of size N, 
with the corresponding times values. This program will draw the trajectory and add a blue sphere,
representing Earth for reference.

For more details see the documentation within the source files (`spacetrace/*`) or the examples (`examples/*`)

## GUI

- Middle mouse button to pan camera
- RMB click to offset camera vertically
- shift + RMB to offset camera horizontally
- Drag slider at the bottom to readjust time
- Left click on entity label (top left) to hide/show
- Press F while hovering over entity label to focus

## License
This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

modules.name: spacetrace._shaders
documentation:
Custom shaders for spacetrace.
vertex and fragment shaders in GLSL 3.3

functions.name: spacetrace.main.show_scene
documentation:
Fire and forget function to show a scene. This will block the main thread until the window is closed.
for args and kwargs, c.f. DrawApplication initializ

classes.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.__init__
documentation:
scene: the scene to be drawn (c.f. spacetrace.scene.Scene)
keyword arguments:
    focus: str
    camera_distance: float
    camera_pitch: float
    camera_yaw: float
    draw_entity_list: bool
        c.f. DrawApplication parameters
    
    show_axes: bool
        Whether to show the default reference frame.
    camera_fov: float
        Field of view of the camera in degrees.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._get_mouse_pos_on_plane
documentation: Projects the mouse position from screen space onto a horizontal plane

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.update_camera
documentation: Updates rotation. Maintains target while updating yaw and pitch trhough mouse input.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.setup
documentation: Setup application after the scene has been fully populated.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.destroy
documentation: Clean up after the application is done to prevent memory leaks.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.set_focus
documentation:
Sets the camera focus to a specific body.
The camera will follow the body through time.
If focus is 'None' or 'DEFAULT_FRAME_NAME', 
the camera will not follow anything.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_trajectories
documentation: Draws all the trajectories through some lower-level graphics shenanigans.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_bodies
documentation: Draws all the bodies in the scene.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_time_bar
documentation: Draws the time slider at the bottom of the screen.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_list_element
documentation: Draws a single element in the list of entities.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_ui
documentation: Draws the rest of the UI, most notably the list of entities.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication._draw_grid
documentation: Draws the primary reference frame and the camera target if no focus is set.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.step
documentation: A single step in the main application loop.

methods.name: spacetrace.main.DrawApplication.is_running
documentation:
Whether or not the application wants to continue running. 
Is false when the window is closed.

Main class for drawing the scene. Handles the core application loop.

Parameters:
-----------
scene: spacetrace.scene.Scene
    Contains all the data and is populated by the user.
focus: str
    Name of the camera focuses on. Defaults to `DEFAULT_FRAME_NAME`.
camera_distance: float
camera_pitch: float
camera_yaw: float
    Spherical coordinates for the camera position relative to the camera target.
    pitch and yaw are in radians.
camera: rl.Camera3D
    Camera object used by raylib.
time_bounds: list[float]
    first and last epochs mentionned by any scene entity.
current_time: float
    Current time, everything is beeing rendered relative to.
draw_entity_list: bool
    Whether to draw the list of entities on the left side of the window.
camera_state: str
    Current state of camera manipulation.
    Can be 'rotating', 'dragging_vertical' or 'dragging_horizontal'.

classes.name: spacetrace.main.show_interactable
documentation:
Context manager to wrap around the scene. This can be called by the user to operate 
the main application loop and gain more control over the rendering or input.

functions.name: spacetrace.scene.default_palette
documentation:
Default color palette for spacetrace.
Simple function that returns the corresponding RGB values for a given color name.
Returns aggressive magenta as error color.

Pallette is a modification of https://lospec.com/palette-list/offshore

functions.name: spacetrace.scene._create_vb_attribute
documentation: Helper function to create vertex buffer attributes

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.Color
documentation: Simple class to handle colors.

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.SceneEntity

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.SceneEntity.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the entity with a name and a color
name: str
    Identifieer used in the UI. Should be unique
color: tuple[float, float, float] or str
    Color of the entity. Can be a tuple of RGB values or a identifies a 
    color in the color palette.
    Default colors are 'main', 'accent', 'bg', 'blue', 'green', 'red', 'white'

Base class for all entities in the scene
Has a name, color, visibility flag as well as a trajectory through time.
SCENE

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.ReferenceFrame
documentation:
The main reference frame in the scene. Currently the scene only has one
automatically created reference frame.

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.Trajectory
documentation:
A trajectory is a sequence of positions in space over time.
Internally, a trajectory can be multiple draw calls. 
This is mostly to access the metadata and to support get_position

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.Body
documentation:
A body is a static or moving object in the scene.
Represented by a colored sphere of a certain radius.
Mostly represents a celestial body.

classes.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the scene with a scale factor. The scale factor is used to convert
provided positions into rendering units. A scale factor of 10^-7 is provided,
assuming that positions are in meters and the trajectories are on the scale of
earth orbits.

Adjust scale_factor, such that the largest dimensions is on the order of magnitude 1-10

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene.add_trajectory
documentation:
Adds a trajectory to the scene. The trajectory is a sequence of states in space over time.
epochs: np.ndarray (N,)
    Time values for each state
states: np.ndarray (N, 3) or (N, 6)
    Position or Positions and velocity states for each time step
    velocities are used to inform the direction of the curve for better rendering
    if velocities are not provided, they are calculated from the positions
name: str
    Identifier used in the UI. Should be unique
color: tuple[float, float, float] or str
    Color of the trajectory. Can be a tuple of RGB values or a identifies a 
    color in the color palette.
    Default colors are 'main', 'accent', 'bg', 'blue', 'green', 'red', 'white'

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene.add_static_body
documentation:
Adds a static body (without trajectory) to the scene. Usefull for central bodies
in a body-centric reference frame.
x: float
y: float
z: float
    Position of the body in space, ususally 0, 0, 0 for central bodies
radius: float
    Radius of the body, in the same units as positions are provided
color: tuple[float, float, float] or str
    Color of the body. Can be a tuple of RGB values or a identifies a 
    color in the color palette.
    Default colors are 'main', 'accent', 'bg', 'blue', 'green', 'red', 'white'

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene.add_moving_body
documentation: Similar to add_static_body, but instear of a single position, a trajectory is provided

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene._add_trajectory_path
documentation: Helper function for add_trajectory. Handle a lot of the low-level rendering setup

methods.name: spacetrace.scene.Scene.entities
documentation: Generates all entities in the scene

All the data that is needed to render a scene in spacetrace
The scene is created and populated by the user.
Entities can be Trajectories, Bodies or the main Reference Frame.
